title: LegalRagBenchQueryOutput
description: Structured output produced by RAG systems for evaluation on Legal RAG Benchmark. Unlike LegalBench-RAG which uses multiple gold_spans, this schema uses a single relevant_passage_id to identify the ground truth passage. The schema maintains compatibility with the RAG evaluation framework while accommodating the Legal RAG Bench data structure.

system_version.description: Version pins for every component that affects output. Required so regression reports can attribute changes to a specific component.

query_id.description: Stable identifier for the query. Eval harness joins on this field to ground truth.

metadata.description: Optional. E.g. RFI category, expected answer type. Passed through from the eval dataset.

text.description: Final generated answer. May be empty string if the system determined no answer is supported by the corpus.

answer_supported.description: System's self-assessment of whether the corpus contains a supporting answer. Distinguishes 'I don't know' from a hallucinated guess.

citations.description: Per-claim citations. Each citation links a span of answer.text to one or more retrieved_chunks.

claim_span.description: [start, end) character offsets into answer.text identifying the cited claim.

chunk_ids.description: chunk_ids from retrieved_chunks[] that support this claim. At least one.

retrieved_chunks.description: All chunks the retriever surfaced for this query, in rank order (rank 0 = highest). Even chunks not used in the answer must be listed — eval needs the full retrieved set to compute recall and precision.

chunk_id.description: Unique within this response. Referenced from answer.citations[].chunk_ids.

score.description: Final retrieval score after any reranking. Higher = more relevant.

retrieval_stage.description: Optional. Distinguishes initial retrieval from post-rerank results when both are reported.

doc_id.description: Joins back to parser_output.source.doc_id or passage_id in Legal RAG Bench corpus.

element_ids.description: element_ids from parser_output.elements[] that this chunk overlaps. Optional but strongly recommended — enables structure-aware eval.

char_span.description: [start, end) character offsets into the source document's full text. This is the canonical evidence locator.

page_indices.description: Optional. Page indices this chunk covers, for human-readable display.

text.description: Verbatim text of the chunk as passed to the generator.

relevant_passage_id.description: Single passage ID from the Legal RAG Bench corpus that contains the answer to the query. Unlike LegalBench-RAG's gold_spans (array of character spans), this is a single passage identifier.

trace.description: Optional. OpenInference / OTel span context for cross-referencing with Phoenix traces.

timings_ms.description: Optional. Per-stage latency in milliseconds, for cost/latency dashboards.
